name: Afternoon Ruby Tea Biscuits
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 425°F (220°C).
2. Measure the sifted flour, baking powder, sugar, and salt and mix with a fork in a large mixing bowl.
3. Cut in the shortening until the mixture looks like coarse bread crumbs.
4. Add milk and mix into flour with the fork, but only until the mixture forms a soft ball.
5. Place the ball of dough on a lightly floured surface and knead it 12 times.
6. Rub some flour on a rolling pin and roll out the dough until it's about 1/4 inch (0.25 cm) thick.
7. With a large biscuit cutter, cut circles very close together in the dough. Use a straight downward motion and do not twist the cutter.
8. With a spatula, lift half the circles, one at a time, onto a cookie sheet. Arrange them about 1 inch (2.5 cm) apart.
9. Using a small biscuit cutter (smaller than the one used to cut out the biscuits), cut a hole in the rest of the circles and lift out the centers with the spatula. Set these little centers aside.
10. Place the rings on top of the large circles on the cookie sheet.
11. Put a teaspoonful of jam or jelly in the middle of each ring.
12. Bake the biscuits for 12 to 15 minutes, or until puffed and slightly golden.
13. Immediately lift the biscuits from the cookie sheet with the spatula.
